targets.name: Card number
targets._description: Use the test card number shown by the payment provider.

penalties: Interop risk: combobox: Combobox behavior varies across AT/browser pairs — verify critical flows with target assistive technologies
16 controls precede this target in control navigation

Cross-AT divergence (HIGH confidence for native <select>, MEDIUM for ARIA combobox): VoiceOver announces this combobox as 'popup button' with state implicit in the role text, while NVDA/JAWS announce 'combo box, expanded/collapsed' explicitly. Verify with real testing if this control is on a critical path.
suggestedFixes: Verify this combobox with the target AT/browser pairs on critical flows; prefer native <select>, <datalist>, or a simpler pattern when custom filtering is not required.
Move this control earlier in the DOM or add a heading anchor nearby

penalties: Target is not efficiently reachable via heading or landmark navigation

Target is 136×21px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum (24×24 for AA). Users with motor impairments have a higher miss rate on small targets. Screen-reader users aren't affected, but touch/pointer users are.
suggestedFixes: Add a heading or landmark near this target to enable skip navigation
Increase min-width and min-height to 24px, or enlarge the clickable area via padding. An invisible padded hit region can enlarge touch target without changing visual design.

diagnostics.message: No skip-to-content link found. Keyboard and screen-reader users must Tab through all navigation elements to reach the main content. A skip link is the single most impactful fix for navigation cost. Concrete savings: a skip link would bypass 3 controls (12% of page interactive targets).
Structural overview: 8 headings · landmarks: main, banner, contentinfo, navigation · skip link: no · 46 total targets
Forms on this page: Form controls (9 fields, 1 required, no submit button).

1 form field of standard input types lack a useful autocomplete attribute (or use autocomplete="off"): email "Email address". Password managers and SR autofill rely on autocomplete tokens (e.g. autocomplete="current-password", autocomplete="email"); without them, users re-type values they shouldn't have to.

2 elements declare an ARIA role but lack the role's required state/property: div#assignee-ada (role="option" missing aria-selected); div#assignee-grace (role="option" missing aria-selected). Without these attributes, screen readers can't communicate the widget's current state to the user (e.g. a checkbox with no aria-checked is unreadable).

5 text elements lose contrast under simulated color-vision deficiency (tritanopia: 5). Examples (tritanopia): a "Form controls" — 9.4:1 normal → 2.44:1 tritanopia (needs 4.5:1); a "Composite widgets" — 9.4:1 normal → 2.44:1 tritanopia (needs 4.5:1); a "Embedded checkout" — 9.4:1 normal → 2.44:1 tritanopia (needs 4.5:1). Approximately 8% of male and 0.5% of female users have a CVD; choose color combinations whose contrast survives the deuteranopia/protanopia/tritanopia transforms or rely on luminance + non-color cues.
